feature.name: Warmage House: Coalition Arcanist
feature.text:
A tactical and technological warmage from the far future, utilizing high technology
Warfare in the 'Verse is a perilous dance of arcana and technology that stretches across entire star systems. In this new theater of combat, none have adapted better than the Coalition and their squads of warmage arcanists. Equipped with specially-designed blaster gauntlets, a coalition arcanist wields magic as others do a blaster: firing off spells automatically, in shotgun-style bursts, and in deadly beams that can level an entire company of foes.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 18 (Indie)

feature.name: Goalition Blaster Gauntlet (Coalition Arcanist)
feature.text:
Starting when you choose this house at 3rd level, you gain a specialized spell focus known as a blaster gauntlet, which allows you to apply some of the traits of blasters to cantrips you cast through it. If the gauntlet is lost or stolen, you can magically requisition another one from the Coalition when you finish a long rest. Whenever you cast a warmage cantrip using your gauntlet as the spellcasting focus, you can use one of the following warmage tricks. You can't use other warmage tricks when you use one of these tricks.
Automatic. When you cast a warmage cantrip that requires a spell attack roll and deals damage, you can decrease the cantrip's damage to make more attacks. Make a number of spell attacks equal to twice the number of damage dice that the cantrip deals. These spell attacks always have disadvantage regardless of circumstance. The cantrip's damage dice are decreased by one step (from a d12 to a d10, a d10 to a d8, and so on, to a minimum of a d4), and each spell attack deals only one of the cantrip's damage dice on a hit.
Nonlethal. When you reduce a creature to 0 hit points using a warmage cantrip that requires a spell attack roll, you can choose to knock the creature out, rendering it unconscious, rather than dealing a killing blow.
Scatter. When you cast a warmage cantrip that deals damage and requires a spell attack roll against a target within 15 feet of you, you can increase the cantrip's damage dice by one step for the attack (from a d4 to a d6, a do to d8, and so on to a maximum of a d12).
Source: Complete Warmage p. 18 (Indie)

feature.name: Tactical Uplink (Coalition Arcanist)
feature.text:
Starting at 7th level, you are fitted with a neural implant, granting you unparalleled battlefield awareness. You can use an action to gain knowledge of the general lay of the land within a 1-mile radius of yourself. This knowledge or artificial) and doesn't detect creatures of any sort. Additionally, you have a +5 bonus to your passive Wisdom (Perception) score.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 18 (Indie)

feature.name: Requisition (Coalition Arcanist)
feature.text:
By 10th level, you can requisition magic items from the Coalition's armory for short-term use. When you finish a long rest, choose one of the following magic items, which is teleported to you: two attractor mines (which use your spell save DC), a dome projector, a gravity nullifier, a jetpack, a ring of spacewalking (which is attuned to you automatically), or a universal translator. This item remains in your possession until you finish a long rest, at which time it is teleported back to the Coalition's armory.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 18 (Indie)

feature.name: Squadsight (Coalition Arcanist)
feature.text:
Starting at 15th level, your tactical uplink synchronizes you with your allies, granting you a decisive tactical advantage. While you are within 120 feet of an ally, you know the exact location of any creature that your ally sees. Additionally, you can use a cantrip to target a creature that your ally can see, as long as that creature isn't in cover from your ally's perspective. This cantrip is fired like a mortar, ricocheted off obstacles, or fired through thin barriers to hit its target.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 18 (Indie)

feature.name: Orbital Bombardment (Coalition Arcanist)
feature.text:
At 18th level, you are granted access to the Coalition's vast fleet of orbital drones, which are teleported to your location. You can cast the spell orbital hardlight cannon without expending a spell slot or material components. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 18 (Indie)

feature.name: Warmage House: House of Darts
feature.text:
Dagger- and dart-throwers that perform increasingly impressive trick shots
Every warmage understands the importance of precision, but the House of Darts flaunts a competitive excellence when it comes to landing shots. Daggers, bolts of magic, and of course, darts are their weapons of choice, but they don't stop at mere bullseyes. A wire array of trick shots set these warmages apart from their contemporaries, even if they lack the grand strategies or discipline of the more martial colleges.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 12 (Indie)

feature.name: Mystical Daggers (House of Darts)
feature.text:
When you choose this house at 3rd level, you learn the magic daggers cantrip, which doesn't count against your number of cantrips known.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 12 (Indie)

feature.name: Trick Shot (House of Darts)
feature.text:
At 3rd level, you can curve your projectiles in mid-air to hit tricky targets. Whenever you make a ranged attack roll with a warmage cantrip or a thrown weapon, you can add or subtract a d4 from the d20 roll (to a maximum of 20 or a minimum of 1). Increasing the d20 roll to a 20 doesn't score a critical hit. You can choose to use this ability after making the attack roll, potentially causing the attack to hit. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 12 (Indie)

feature.name: Bullseye (House of Darts)
feature.text:
Also at 3rd level, you can sink a shot exactly where you need to. Record the number rolled on the d20 whenever you make a ranged spell attack roll with a warmage cantrip. Rolls that have advantage and disadvantage record the higher and lower d20 roll, respectively. If a d20 roll is increased or decreased using your Trick Shot feature, use the new number. A recorded roll lasts for 1 minute. If you roll one of your recorded numbers again when making a spell attack roll with a warmage cantrip, you gain one of the following benefits of your choice:
• You can reroll the d20 and must use the new roll. Record this new d20 roll. • You can deal an extra 1d10 force damage to the target. • You regain an expended use of your Trick Shot feature.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 12 (Indie)

feature.name: Flurry of Blades (House of Darts)
feature.text:
Beginning at 7th level, when you cast a cantrip that allows you to make multiple spell attacks, such as force weapon or magic daggers, you can use your bonus action to make one additional spell attack with that cantrip.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 12 (Indie)

feature.name: Intercepting Shot (House of Darts)
feature.text:
Starting at 10th level, as a reaction when an attacker you can see within 60 feet makes an attack against you or a friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you, you can make a ranged spell attack using magic daggers to interrupt the attack. If the result of your roll is greater than the result of the attacker, you can reduce the attack roll targeting you by 5 (to a minimum result of 1).
Source: Complete Warmage p. 12 (Indie)

feature.name: Vanishing Toss (House of Darts)
feature.text:
At 15th level, you master the house's signature technique for recovering darts. Whenever you cast the magic daggers cantrip, you can replace one of your spell attacks with a special Vanishing Toss that embeds a magic dagger in an object you can see within range. You can use your bonus action to teleport to the dagger as long as you are within 500 feet of it. The dagger disappears after 10 minutes or if you use this ability to throw another Vanishing Toss.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 12 (Indie)

feature.name: Dart Burst (House of Darts)
feature.text:
Beginning at 18th level, if you have recorded ten or more unique numbers using your Bullseye feature, you can use your action to clear all of your recorded numbers and throw a burst of darts. Each creature in a 60-foot cone must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes 1d8 force damage for each unique recorded number on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 12 (Indie)

feature.name: Warmage House: House of Go
feature.text:
Strategists that use formations of allies to surround and crush their foes
The renowned House of Go predates the other warmage colleges by centuries. Unlike its successors, the strategies pioneered by the House of Go focus upon formations and cooperation, rather than individual excellence. Which isn't to suggest that warmages of this college are anything other than brilliant strategists-they rival the House of Kings in this regard. Rather, the footsoldiers of the House of Go are greater than the sum of their parts, fluidly adopting motions to isolate and flank their foes.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 13 (Indie)

feature.name: Formations (House of Go)
feature.text:
Starting when you choose this house at 3rd level, you learn special tactics called Formations. You can use a bonus action to activate one of the following Formations, which lasts until you and your allies are no longer in that formation, you are incapacitated, die, or you use this feature to activate a different formation.
Flank. You can use this formation when you and an ally that isn't incapacitated are adjacent to a hostile creature and on opposite sides of it. You can determine this by tracing a 5-foot-wide line from your space. If this line passes through the center of all three spaces, the hostile creature is flanked. You and your ally have advantage on attack rolls against the flanked creature.
Solo. You can use this formation when you have no allies within 5 feet of you. While in this formation, if you target a creature with a cantrip, that creature can't make opportunity attacks against you for the rest of the turn or until you are no longer in this formation, whether you deal damage with the cantrip or not.
Wall. You can use this formation when you and two to six allies that aren't incapacitated are within 5 feet of each other. Each creature in this formation (including you, gains a +2 bonus to its Armor Class.
Flanking Rules: If your table already uses optional flanking rules, using the Flanking formation gains an additional benefit: you and your ally also have advantage on saving throws you make against effects caused by the flanked creature.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 13 (Indie)

feature.name: Opening Moves (House of Go)
feature.text:
Starting at 7th level, you can spend 10 minutes planning an encounter in exact detail. When you do so, you can share your plan with up to six allies within 30 feet of you who can hear or see you and who can understand you. Each ally that understands the plan can gain advantage on an attack roll or ability check it makes within the next 10 minutes. If an ally rolls initiative during this time, it can't use this feature to gain advantage after the end of the first round of combat. Once you use this feature to make a plan, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 13 (Indie)

feature.name: Hardened Formations (House of Go)
feature.text:
Beginning at 10th level, while you are in a formation, any critical hit against you or an ally in your formation becomes a normal hit.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 14 (Indie)

feature.name: Chain of Liberties (House of Go)
feature.text:
By 15th level, your formation moves as if your allies were links in a chain. Whenever you move on your turn, you can spend movement to allow willing allies in your formation to move to locations you choose. You must spend movement separately for each ally you wish to move.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 14 (Indie)

feature.name: End Game (House of Go)
feature.text:
By 18th level, you can play out the last few moves of the game in rapid succession. On your turn, when you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with a warmage cantrip, you can cast another cantrip as a bonus action. You can apply Warmage Edge to this cantrip, even if you have already used it on your turn.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 14 (Indie)

feature.name: Warmage House: House of Roulette
feature.text:
A gambler that cashes in chips on a roulette table to enhance their cantrips
Like the Houses of Dice and Cards, warmages of the House of Roulette are masters of the calculated gamble. Bearing a number of magical chips, they can push the odds when the need arises, pulling off longshot plays that other warmages could only dream of.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 17 (Indie)

feature.name: Spin the Wheel (House of Roulette)
feature.text:
Starting when you choose this house at 3rd level, you gain a roulette ball infused with arcane energy and a number of chips. As a bonus action, you can conjure a roulette wheel of magical force and roll the ball. Choose one of the bets from the Roulette Bets table below, announce your guess, and roll a d100. You gain the listed benefit if your guess is correct.
Chips of Fate. You have four Chips of Fate, and regain all expended chips when you finish a long rest. You gain two additional Chips of Fate at 7th level (6 chips total), two more at 10th level (8 chips total), and two more at 15th level (10 chips total). Whenever you place a bet on the roulette wheel, you can expend one to four of your chips to place additional bets equal to the number of chips expended. No matter how many chips you expend, you can only place two types of bets, such as Singles and Tens, per d100 roll. You can't place more than one Odd/Even or High/Low bets. You can gain two benefits at once if you win two bets on the same roll.
Roulette Bets: Bet | Benefit Odd/Even | Guess whether the roll is even or odd. | You gain a +2 bonus to your AC until the start of your next turn. High/Low | Guess whether the roll is above or below 50. | You gain a +2 bonus on attack rolls you make until the start of your next turn. Lanes | Guess which of the following spans the number falls within: 01-20, 21-40, 41-60, 61-80, 81-00. | You can gain advantage on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw you make before the end of your next turn. Tens | Guess the 10s digit of the roll. | Once before the end of your next turn, you can add 10 to the damage roll of a warmage spell you cast. Single | Guess the exact number of the roll. | Until the start of your next turn, you have resistance to all damage and treat any d20 roll you make as 20.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 17 (Indie)

feature.name: Lucky Number (House of Roulette)
feature.text:
Beginning at 7th level, you can thumb the scales of fortune in favor of a longshot bet. Pick a Lucky Number from 1 to 100. If you roll that number on any d100 roll of your Spin the Wheel feature, you gain the benefit of betting that number as a Single. You can change your Lucky Number whenever you finish a long rest.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 17 (Indie)

feature.name: Spinning Shield (House of Roulette)
feature.text:
Starting at 10th level, as a reaction when a creature you can see makes an attack roll against you, you can conjure your roulette wheel between yourself and the attacker. You and the attacker each roll a d100. If your roll is equal or higher, the attack misses, and you can't use this ability again until you finish a short or long rest.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 17 (Indie)

feature.name: Gash Out (House of Roulette)
feature.text:
By 15th level, you can unleash the energy stored within a Chip of Fate to escape a tight spot. As a bonus action, you can expend a Chip of Fate to teleport up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 17 (Indie)

feature.name: Everything on Black (House of Roulette)
feature.text:
Starting at 18th level, you regain 4 expended Chips of Fate whenever you roll initiative. Additionally, whenever you expend one or more Chips of Fate to bet on a Single and win, you regain all of your expended Chips of Fate.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 18 (Indie)

spell.name: Warmage Trick: Blackjack (HB)
spell.text:
Prerequisite: House of Cards, House f Darts, House of Dice, or House of Roulette
When you cast a cantrip that requires a single spell attack roll and you don't have disadvantage on the roll, you can choose to roll with advantage. The attack automatically misses if the sum of the two rolled dice exceeds 21, and it is a critical hit if the sum equals 21.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 5 (Indie)

spell.name: Warmage Trick: Gontingent Cantrip (HB)
spell.text:
Prerequisite: House of Cards, House f Darts, House of Dice, or House of Roulette
When you finish a long rest, you can spend 10 minutes performing a ritual to create a contingent cantrip. Cast a warmage cantrip that has a casting time of 1 action or 1 bonus action and doesn't deal damage. The cantrip doesn't take effect immediately, but instead it takes effect the next time you speak a specified command word (no action required). When you perform this ritual, choose whether the cantrip targets you, the nearest friendly creature, or the nearest hostile creature. The cantrip can't use other warmage tricks when it comes into effect. You can only have one contingent cantrip at a time. If you perform this ritual again, the previous contingent cantrip ends.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 5 (Indie)

spell.name: Warmage Trick: Magic Moment (HB)
spell.text:
Prerequisite: moment to think cantrip
When you cast moment to think, you can choose to also stop time for a willing or unconscious creature you can see within 30 feet. The creature can take one of the actions permitted by the spell during your turn, and you can interact with one another while time is stopped. During this time you can exchange items, swap locations, take the Help action for tasks while time is stopped, attempt to stabilize a dying creature, or have a short conversation without eavesdropping or interruption.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 6 (Indie)

spell.name: Warmage Trick: Mystical Retort (HB)
spell.text:
You learn the vicious mockery cantrip, which doesn't count against your number of cantrips known. Additionally, when a creature you can see makes an attack roll against you, you can cast vicious mockery targeting that creature as a reaction. If the creature fails its saving throw, the cantrip imposes disadvantage on the triggering attack roll, instead of the creature's next attack roll, potentially causing the attack to miss. Once you cast this cantrip as a reaction, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 6 (Indie)

spell.name: Warmage Trick: Prismatic Cantrip (HB)
spell.text:
Once on each of your turns, when you deal force damage with a warmage cantrip, you can roll a d6 on the following table to change the cantrip's damage type for this casting. You can apply tricks to the casting as normal using the new damage type. Additionally, you can apply the trick corresponding to the rolled damage type, even if you don't know the trick. You can do so twice and regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.
Prismatic Cantrip: d6 | Damage Type | Warmage Trick 1 | acid | Corrosive Cantrip 2 | cold | Icy Cantrip 3 | fire | Explosive Cantrip 4 | lightning | Static Cantrip 5 | poison | Draining Cantrip 6 | thunder | Silent Cantrip
Source: Complete Warmage p. 6 (Indie)

spell.name: Warmage Trick: Radiant Cantrip (HB)
spell.text:
Once on each of your turns, when you cast a spell that deals acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, poison, or thunder, you can change the damage type to radiant. You can reroll one of the cantrip's damage dice and must use the new roll. The number of damage dice you can reroll increases by 1 when you reach 11th level (2 dice) and 17th level (3 dice).
Source: Complete Warmage p. 6 (Indie)

spell.name: Warmage Trick: Residual Cantrip (HB)
spell.text:
When you cast a cantrip that deals damage and requires a single spell attack roll and you miss, you can channel some of the residual magic into a secondary beam of arcane energy as a bonus action. Make a ranged spell attack against a creature you can see within the cantrip's range. On a hit, the target takes 1d6 damage of the cantrip's damage type. This beam doesn't count as a cantrip. This damage increases to 1d8 when you reach 5th level, to 1d10 when you reach 11th level, and to 1d12 when you reach 17th level.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 6 (Indie)

spell.name: Warmage Trick: Capture (HB)
spell.text:
Prerequisite: 10th level Warmage (Valda), House of Go You gain the following formation:
Capture. You can use this formation when you and three or more allies that aren't incapacitated are adjacent to a hostile creature and surround it. You can determine this by placing an imaginary cube around the hostile creature. If you and your allies are adjacent to each side of the cube, the creature is surrounded. When you activate this formation, the hostile creature must make a Dexterity saving throw against your warmage spell save DC or be enveloped by chains of magical force and stunned for 1 minute. At the end of each of the creatures turns, it can make a Dexterity saving throw, freeing itself and ending this formation on a success. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 6 (Indie)

spell.name: Warmage Trick: Chips Are Down (HB)
spell.text:
Prerequisite: 10th level Warmage (Valda), House of Roulette
When you expend one or more Chips of Fate to bet and lose at least one bet, you regain one expended Chips of Fate.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 6 (Indie)

spell.name: Warmage Trick: Dartmaster (HB)
spell.text:
Prerequisite: 10th level Warmage (Valda), House of Darts
When you cast a cantrip that allows you to make multiple ranged spell attacks, such as magic daggers or force dart using the Split Fire trick, and you miss with one or more attacks, you can reroll all of your misses. Once you use this trick, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 6 (Indie)

spell.name: Warmage Trick: Gauntlet Upgrade (HB)
spell.text:
Prerequisite: 10th level (Warmage), Coalition Arcanist
You install a high-power module in your blaster gauntlet. Whenever you cast a warmage cantrip using your gauntlet as the spellcasting focus, you can use one of the following warmage tricks. You can't use other warmage tricks when you use one of these tricks. Once you use one of these tricks, you can't use that trick again until you finish a long rest.
Beam. When you cast a warmage cantrip that requires one or more spell attack rolls and deals damage, you can focus the cantrip into a 5-foot-wide, 60-footlong line. Each creature in the line must make a Dexterity saving throw against your warmage spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d10 damage for each point of your proficiency bonus. The damage type is the same type that the cantrip deals. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage. You can't use your Warmage Edge or Arcane Surge features when you use this trick.
Overheat. The cantrip's damage dice become d12s.
Source: Complete Warmage p. 6 (Indie)
